instruction:
The melting speed of half of the spherical snowdrift is directly proportional to the surface area of the hemisphere, and the proportional coefficient is $k > 0$. Suppose that the shape remains unchanged during the melting process, and suppose that the volume of the snowdrift with radius $r_{0}$is the original $\frac{1} {8}$after melting for 3 hours, and the time required for full melting is ().

Options:
A. $6$ hours
B. $9$ hours
C. $24$ hours
D. $15$ hours
E. $3$ hours
F. $1$ hours
G. $12$ hours
H. $18$ hours
I. $2$ hours
J. $21$ hours
